title: podman save --format with subsequent podman load -i changes image digest
body:
### Issue Description

It seems that `podman save --format` and a subsequent `podman load -i` with format being either `docker-dir` or `oci-dir` changes the digest locally. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
podman pull quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal@sha256:2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46
podman save --format=oci-dir ./2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46 quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal@sha256:2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46
podman image prune --all -f
podman --root=$(pwd)/2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46/imagestore load -i ./2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46
podman --root=$(pwd)/2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46/imagestore tag localhost/var/apps/2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46 quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal

podman --root=$(pwd)/2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46/imagestore image ls --digests
REPOSITORY                                                                           TAG         DIGEST                                                                   IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE
localhost/var/apps/2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46  latest      sha256:34248321781ba0640b043ab46479e7077c9e190535f269d70bf12f34f53f4389  61eac1b08399  6 hours ago  104 MB
quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal                                                        latest      sha256:34248321781ba0640b043ab46479e7077c9e190535f269d70bf12f34f53f4389  61eac1b08399  6 hours ago  104 MB

jq -r '.manifests[0].annotations."org.opencontainers.image.ref.name"' $(pwd)/index.json
quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal@sha256:2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46
```

### Describe the results you received

The following command returns another digest:

```
jq -r '.manifests[0].digest' $(pwd)/index.json
sha256:34248321781ba0640b043ab46479e7077c9e190535f269d70bf12f34f53f4389
```

### Describe the results you expected

The digest of the image on the container registry should be equalt to `.manifests[0].digest`

comments.body: This issue looks somewhat familiar to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/3761
Perhaps hopefully @vrothberg and @mtrmac will respond. Valentin is on PTO til monday.

`quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal@sha256:2241a840543ec303b48eeda1a42168338fa92dec8fc94b65d437a932ebfd3a46` is
- multiarch; that’s lost by `podman pull`, which only chooses one per-platform instance.
- not OCI (only OCI-formatted images can be stored in OCi archives); the original image is Docker schema2 and had to be converted.
- compressed on the registry; decompressed by `podman pull`. The original compressed format can’t be locally recreated.

_One_ of that would be sufficient to cause the manifest in the archive created by `podman save` to differ from the on-registry manifest. It just fundamentally isn’t possible to recreate a compressed artifact (without a frozen and explicitly-designed-to-be-reproducible compression implementation).

(And yes, then there’s #3761 about the difficulties we have with tracking `RepoDigests` and the like. But we don’t even get to that part here.)

If you want to move around the original compressed artifact and to preserve its binary representation, use `skopeo copy` for direct copies, and don’t “pull”+“push”; a pull is inherently lossy from this perspective. And probably use direct registry-to-registry copies, or perhaps `dir:`, not `oci:`, unless the input archive is known to be in the OCI format already.

@mtrmac Thanks for the prompt response and detailed explaination. Using `skopeo copy` solved the issue so I would argue that is used podman pull + save in the wrong way and this issue does not classify as a bug. I will go ahead and close this issue.
